session_note: S70 (user "go" — autonomous Full run on the S69-carried P3). P1 = lobby presence broadcast, Council-vetted (Claude+Grok+Gemini, 2-round), net-free, NO version bump. Host broadcasts LOBBY_PRESENCE{roster:RosterEntry[]} on peer join/leave + LOCAL self-dispatch (send() excludes host); joiner recv -> event-driven onPresence thunk -> lobbyScreen.updatePresence -> reducer PRESENCE -> lobbyView derives seats from real roster (own-seat glow via peerId===selfId), count-based fallback. Reducer stays net-free (digest RosterEntry->SeatPresence in wiring).
council_battle_ledger: S70 P1 Full -- 3-way Council (Claude+Grok+Gemini), R1 Trident + R2 Claude convergence, Quality Gate PASS. Signals: novel_mechanism (new LOBBY_PRESENCE kind, +1rd absorbed at ceiling), external_user_facing (lobby UI -> Gemini required). FORKS: A=event-driven onPresence thunk (UNANIMOUS); C=digest RosterEntry->render-local SeatPresence, reducer net-free (UNANIMOUS); B=protocol version SPLIT -> CONCEDED to GROK x1.75 risk authority = NO BUMP (LOBBY_PRESENCE is cosmetic; v4 straggler null-rejects+falls back to count-based, can still play; bump would hard-reject at HELLO for a cosmetic msg; prior bumps protected gameplay desync, N/A here; bonus zero version-test churn). ADOPTED: host LOCAL self-dispatch (Grok R6 CRIT -- send() excludes self, draft would have shipped a host rack that never updates); reuse START_GAME_SIGNAL roster validator in parseNetMessage (Grok logic, DRY); compute SeatPresence once per PRESENCE event (Grok logic); buildLobbyRoster pure+capped at MAX_PLAYERS, reused at Begin (DRY); strict gating gameState===LOBBY + reducer ignores PRESENCE in select; minimal P#/crown via P2 existing label/badge slots (Gemini). SYNTHESIS: no debounce (join/leave human-paced; churn-guard absorbs redundant rosters); seats compact on leave (consistent w/ Begin, in-scope). REJECTED w/ reasons: Grok local-derivation (join-order seat authority is host-only; sorted-peerId would diverge from Begin), targetedSend (premature at 6 peers; note as Phase-3 >6), remove count-based/PEER_STATUS (it IS the fallback + Begin-enable gate). DEFERRED (logged, NOT dropped): stable non-compacting seats (needs Begin authoritative path = out of net-free scope); join/leave fill animations (=P2 D1). Confidence HIGH.
prime_audit: S70 P1 PRIME-AUDIT: synthesis materially > R1 -- Council caught a real HOST-SELF-VIEW bug (transport.send() broadcasts to PEERS not self, so the host rack would never update from its own broadcast; FIX = host calls onPresence locally) AND flipped Fork B (bump->no-bump) on a decisive cosmetic-vs-gameplay argument. NOT rubber-stamped: changed position on B, added host-local-dispatch, rejected 3 Grok alternatives with reasons. Runtime-verifiability: boot-smoke (host+joiner own-seat glow, leave->drop) + targeted multi-peer Playwright (e2e is tsc/knip-blind per gotcha -- MUST run). Net-free verified: no change to START_GAME_SIGNAL shape / authoritative seating / determinism; LOBBY_PRESENCE is lobby-cosmetic only. selfId identity-match (peerId===selfId) is the same shipped S62 assumption clientHandlers already relies on at Begin. R5 broadcast-loss downgraded CRIT->LOW (cosmetic; next join/leave re-broadcasts self-heals; Begin authoritative). Confidence HIGH.
triumvirate_check: S70 P1 Full CHECK = Triumvirate (RALPH:PATROL + GROK-ANALYST + GEMINI-AUDITOR, adversarial distinct lenses). RALPH PASS (spec-conformance; PDR contract + 5 verification[] bindings met). GEMINI-AUDITOR PASS (Fidelity 5/Quality 5/Completeness 5; objective closed, no regression vs P2's count-based rack). GROK-ANALYST FAIL -> Opus inline triage (NOT rubber-stamped): joiner-spoof is cosmetic + consistent with the pre-existing unauthenticated trust model (START_GAME_SIGNAL/ENDGAME equally unauth, far more impactful) + v1 friends-only scope -> documented; the no-length-cap sub-finding FIXED (<=MAX_PLAYERS clamp, both kinds); host-loss-stale = pre-existing netcode backlog + the S69-reverted peerCount=0 anti-pattern (deliberate no-action); race REFUTED (LOBBY gate + stable peerIds order). NET: SHIP (2/3 PASS, FIX-THEN-SHIP done). Grok 2 calls (R1 plan + CHECK), Gemini 2 calls (R1 plan + CHECK) ~$0.20.

priorities.label: S70 P1 (Full) -- Lobby presence broadcast (Council-vetted, net-free, NO version bump). SCOPE (~9 files): protocol.ts add LOBBY_PRESENCE{roster:RosterEntry[]} + NetMessage union + fail-closed parseNetMessage case reusing the START_GAME_SIGNAL roster validator (NO PROTOCOL_VERSION bump -- cosmetic msg, v4 peer null-rejects+falls back); hostHandlers.ts extract pure buildLobbyRoster(peerIds,selfId) capped MAX_PLAYERS (reuse at Begin, DRY) + broadcast LOBBY_PRESENCE on onPeerChange join/leave + LOCAL onPresence self-dispatch (send() excludes host); clientHandlers.ts recv LOBBY_PRESENCE while gameState===LOBBY -> onPresence; lobbyStateMachine.ts PRESENCE event + presenceRoster (render-local SeatPresence, NO net import) + lobbyView roster-derivation (own-seat glow peerId===selfId, real color) w/ count-based fallback, reset on HOST_START/JOIN_ATTEMPT/RESET, same-ref churn-guard, ignore in select; lobbyScreen.ts updatePresence(roster,selfId) -> dispatch PRESENCE + render glow/color (KEEP all __SPARK__ accessors + shell verbatim; minimal P#/crown via P2 slots); main.ts wire onPresence thunk (host+client); protocol.test.ts LOBBY_PRESENCE parse (valid+malformed fail-closed + v4 graceful-reject); lobbyStateMachine.test.ts PRESENCE derivation+fallback+churn; NEW buildLobbyRoster unit test + e2e/ targeted multi-peer presence (joiner own-seat glow + leave drop) RUN Playwright. DEFERRED: stable non-compacting seats (needs Begin path), join/leave animations. GATE: tsc -b --noEmit PASS + knip 0 + vitest (1013+new) + vite build <=550KB + self-driven boot-smoke + Playwright gating+multi-peer. CHECK: Triumvirate (RALPH+GROK+GEMINI adversarial) + Rule22.
priorities.check_method: S70 P1 (lobby presence broadcast, Full, Council-vetted, net-free, NO version bump) VERIFIED + SHIPPED 57ee6b1. Host broadcasts LOBBY_PRESENCE{roster:RosterEntry[]} on peer join/leave + LOCAL self-dispatch (transport.send excludes self -- Grok R6 CRIT, the draft would have shipped a host rack that never updates from its own broadcast); joiner recv (gameState===LOBBY gate) -> event-driven onPresence thunk -> main.ts digest (isYou=peerId===selfId) -> lobbyScreen.updatePresence -> reducer PRESENCE -> lobbyView roster-derived seats (own-seat glow) with count-based fallback. NEW pure buildLobbyRoster reused at Begin (DRY) + lobbyRoster.test. Reducer stays net-free (SeatPresence render-local, Fork C). NO PROTOCOL_VERSION bump (Fork B: cosmetic; a v4 peer null-rejects + falls back; zero version-test churn). seatRack.ts/lobbyGeometry.ts UNCHANGED (P2 render already roster-ready). GATES: tsc -b PASS; knip 0; vitest 1039 (+26 vs S69: buildLobbyRoster 7, reducer PRESENCE+roster-view 13, protocol parse+clamp 6); vite build 512.54KB (<550 charter). RUNTIME: Playwright gating 24/24 (incl the new deterministic presence test + the lobby-construction net that caught the P2 regression + 3-peer real-WebRTC smoke) + a real-2-peer-WebRTC presence test PASS (joiner sees own seat 1 + host self-dispatch seat 0, both pre-Begin in LOBBY). CHECK Triumvirate (adversarial): RALPH:PATROL PASS (spec-conformance, contract + all 5 verification[] bindings met); GEMINI-AUDITOR PASS 5/5/5 (objective closed, no regression vs P2); GROK-ANALYST FAIL -> Opus inline triage: (1) joiner-spoof REAL but cosmetic-only + consistent with the pre-existing unauthenticated control-message trust model (START_GAME_SIGNAL/ENDGAME are equally unauth + affect REAL game state) + the codebase v1 friends-only threat scope -> documented limitation, NOT a P3-introduced gameplay vuln; the actionable no-length-cap sub-finding FIXED (added <=MAX_PLAYERS clamp to isValidRoster, fail-closed + bounds receive-side Map-build DoS, hardens BOTH message kinds); (2) host-loss-stale ~= pre-P3 count-clamp behavior + reacting to peerCount=0 is the exact S69-P2-reverted anti-pattern -> deliberate non-action, real fix is the pre-existing connection-lost/host-loss netcode backlog; (3) race-vs-START_GAME REFUTED (gameState===LOBBY gate ignores a late beacon; peerIds insertion-order == the Begin order). NET VERDICT 2/3 PASS = SHIP, FIX-THEN-SHIP satisfied (clamp). Rule22 runtime audit CLEAN (pure TS game-code; no env-VAR placeholders / Docker / CLI-flags / restart-paths; all imports resolve per tsc; real-browser Playwright IS the boot-then-smoke). checkpoint 57ee6b1. CI (deploy + e2e) triggers on push -- watched in handoff Rule22.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #lobby-presence-broadcast #council-full #no-version-bump #host-self-dispatch #grok-fail-triaged #fork-flip: shipped the S69-carried P3 -- a host->peer LOBBY_PRESENCE{roster} beacon on each peer join/leave so JOINERS finally see their OWN lobby seat (own-seat glow + real per-seat colour + accurate drop-on-leave), closing P2's count-based limits (Gemini-C1 joiner-isYou + Grok-1 drop accuracy). DEFINING MOVES: (1) State-discovery (Rule21) BEFORE locking the PDR found the S69 design memory was STALE -- reading the ACTUAL post-P2 code showed the render (seatRack SeatView) was ALREADY roster-ready, so P3 = a pure DATA-path change (no render edit) = smaller than the carried estimate; seatRack.ts/lobbyGeometry.ts untouched. (2) The Council FLIPPED my draft on Fork B: I leaned bump-the-protocol-version (house precedent), Grok (x1.75 risk authority) argued NO-bump because LOBBY_PRESENCE is COSMETIC (the authoritative roster still ships at Begin) so a stale peer null-rejects+falls-back rather than being hard-rejected at HELLO for a non-gameplay message -- decisive, conceded; bonus = zero version-test churn. (3) The Council caught a REAL bug pre-impl: transport.send() broadcasts to PEERS not self, so the host's own rack would never update from its own broadcast -> host must call onPresence LOCALLY (Grok R6). (4) CHECK GROK-ANALYST returned FAIL (joiner-spoof) -- triaged adversarially, NOT accepted wholesale: the spoof is cosmetic + consistent with the codebase's pre-existing unauthenticated control-message trust model (START_GAME_SIGNAL/ENDGAME are equally unauth + affect REAL game state) + the explicit v1 friends-only threat scope, so it is NOT a P3-introduced gameplay vuln; kept + FIXED only the sound sub-finding (<=MAX_PLAYERS clamp on isValidRoster: fail-closed + bounds a Map-build DoS); refuted the race (gameState===LOBBY gate) + host-loss-stale (= the S69-P2-reverted peerCount=0 anti-pattern). LESSON: a Council/CHECK FAIL on a cosmetic feature is triaged against the EXISTING trust model + threat scope, not accepted wholesale -- adopt the cheap sound fix, document the rest as LOGGED limitations (never silently dropped). Gates: tsc, knip 0, vitest 1039, build 512.54KB; Playwright gating 24/24 + real-2-peer presence pass. checkpoint 57ee6b1.
end_of_session_audit: S70 P1 Rule22 runtime audit CLEAN: the diff is pure TS game-code + tests -- no env-VAR placeholders, no Docker or deploy artifacts, no CLI flags, no restart-survival paths. All cross-module imports resolve (tsc -b PASS is the static module-resolution proof). The runtime complement to CHECK's static pass = real-browser Playwright (24 gating + a real-2-peer-WebRTC presence flow) = a genuine boot-then-smoke, not static-parse. Post-push CI (deploy + e2e) re-check pending -- to confirm in the S70 handoff Rule22 pass.
post_ship_analysis: S70 P1 post-ship: live on spark-online.space (deploy CI conclusion=success); E2E CI conclusion=success (gating lane 24/24; the quarantine-lane exit-1 annotation is the by-design continue-on-error real-WebRTC flake, NOT a regression). No regressions. Calibration-debt watch (rolling): Grok CHECK severity over-escalation recurs (S66-68 FUD-refuted pattern); this session the findings were substantive but the FAIL verdict over-weighted out-of-scope/cosmetic edges -> mitigation queued in analyze #improve (put threat-model scope in the CHECK prompt). Next session: re-check whether that prompt change reduces Grok-CHECK over-escalation.
carry_forward: === RESOLVED S70 === P1 (57ee6b1) lobby presence broadcast: host->peer LOBBY_PRESENCE on peer join/leave + local self-dispatch; joiners get the true per-seat roster (own-seat glow, real colour, accurate drop-on-leave); net-free, NO protocol version bump; Council Full + Triumvirate SHIP. Closed P2's Gemini-C1 (joiner-isYou) + Grok-1 (drop accuracy). === DEFERRED (logged by the S70 Council/CHECK, NOT dropped) === (a) STABLE non-compacting lobby seats (a peer keeps its seat even when an earlier peer leaves) -- Council-recommended (Grok+Gemini convergent) but needs touching the AUTHORITATIVE Begin seating path, out of P3's net-free scope; current behavior = seats COMPACT on leave, consistent with Begin. (b) Lobby join/leave fill ANIMATIONS (Gemini Living-Lobby, = P2's deferred D1) -- polish, adds Pixi tweening. (c) PRESENCE sender-authentication: LOBBY_PRESENCE (like the pre-existing START_GAME_SIGNAL/ENDGAME) is trusted from any peer -- harmless under the v1 friends-only threat model + cosmetic, but a future adversarial-peer-resistance pass should add host-peerId verification across ALL control messages (broader than P3). (d) Lobby host-LOSS UX: a joiner whose host disconnects keeps a stale presence rack behind the connection-lost path -- part of the pre-existing netcode backlog (host-migration/reconnect). === OTHER (pre-existing, unchanged) === EYES: fog fuzzy-edge + CVD shape-icons; LIVE-PLAY netcode infra (host-migration/reconnect/6p delta/3+-player host-loss); main.ts Pixi/DOM shell hypertrophy.
analyze_summary: S70 ANALYZE (Claude-ZERO retrospective; solo-led -- proportionate for 1 clean Full priority, calibration computed from the session record rather than 2 more API self-assessment calls). METHOD EFFECTIVENESS: HIGH -- the 3-way Council demonstrably earned its keep: it FLIPPED my draft Fork B (bump->no-bump, Grok x1.75 risk authority, decisive cosmetic-vs-gameplay argument) AND caught a REAL pre-impl bug (transport.send excludes self -> host-self-dispatch R6; my draft would have shipped a host rack that never updates). State-discovery (Rule21) BEFORE the PDR caught stale S69 design memory (render was already roster-ready) -> right-sized scope (pure data-path, no render edit). CALIBRATION: Claude(Architect) R1 had 2 real misses (Fork B lean + missed host-self-dispatch), both Council-corrected = Council ROI proven. Grok(Disruptor) PLAN adoption HIGH (no-bump + R6 + DRY-validator + compute-once adopted; local-derivation/targetedSend/remove-fallback rejected-with-reason); CHECK findings SUBSTANTIVE not FUD (spoof REAL-but-out-of-scope, length-cap GENUINE+fixed, host-loss REAL-but-pre-existing) but the FAIL verdict OVER-ESCALATED severity on cosmetic/out-of-scope edges (race was a miss -- it ignored the gameState===LOBBY gate). Gemini(Auditor) Fork votes sound; CHECK PASS 5/5/5 well-calibrated; but PLAN UX advocacy STALE (advocated host-crown/P# labels that P2 ALREADY shipped -- lacked current-UI context). SCOPE DISCIPLINE: excellent (net-free held; stable-seats/animations/sender-auth/host-loss all DEFERRED+logged; only in-scope add = the CHECK-driven length clamp). TOKEN: 389K/1M (38.94% GREEN) for a full Full priority + 4 Council calls + impl + tests + 2 Playwright runs -- efficient (state-discovery prevented wasted work). #worked: state-discovery-before-PDR; adversarial-CHECK-triage (scoped Grok's FAIL against the existing trust model, neither rubber-stamped nor accepted wholesale); deterministic-gates + quarantine-real-WebRTC e2e split (matched Council DP1). #improve: (1) feed Gemini the CURRENT shipped-UI state when consulting on UX so it does not advocate for already-shipped features; (2) include the explicit threat-model/scope (v1 friends-only) in Grok's CHECK prompt so its FAIL-severity calibrates to scope rather than over-escalating out-of-scope edges.
